content: Volume of distribution (Vd) is a theoretical volume relating the amount of drug in the body to its plasma concentration: Vd = Amount of drug / Plasma concentration. It indicates the extent of drug distribution beyond plasma into tissues. Vd does not represent a real physiologic volume but reflects tissue affinity. Small Vd (~3-5L, plasma volume): drugs stay in circulation (warfarin, heparin - highly protein bound). Moderate Vd (~14L, extracellular fluid): aminoglycosides. Large Vd (>40L, total body water or more): extensive tissue distribution (digoxin Vd=500L, chloroquine Vd=15,000L). High Vd drugs are poorly removed by dialysis.
